structured.description: A `Shader` class that loads and manages shaders for opengl rendering. It uses the `GL20` package for opengl functionality. The class provides methods for binding the program, getting the program id, uniform floats, and matrix 4f. It also includes a method to load shaders from files and validate the program.

items.description: in Java provides a framework for creating and managing shaders for use in OpenGL applications. It allows for the creation of vertex and fragment shaders, as well as the binding and uniform functions to manipulate data within these shaders. Additionally, the class provides methods for loading and validating shaders, as well as utility methods for handling input streams.
items.name: Shader

items.description: glues a program to a set of data.

usage.code:
Shader shader = Shader.loadShader("texture");
shader.bind();

items.name: bind

items.description: retrieves the value of a field named `program`.

returns.description: an integer value representing the program.

items.name: getProgram

items.description: is a method that sets a uniform float value to a specific location in a GL context based on the length of the input array.

params.name: name
params.description: name of the uniform location in the GL context, which is used to determine the correct uniform function to call in the switch statement.

usage.description: In this example, the program will take the float value of 10 and send it to a uniform variable named "myFloat" in the shader.

items.name: uniformf

items.description: sets a 4x4 matrix as a uniform variable in OpenGL context using `glUniformMatrix4`.

params.name: name
params.description: 0-based index of the uniform location where the matrix will be stored.

params.name: mat
params.description:
4x4 matrix to be uniformed.

* `name`: A string representing the uniform name.
* `mat`: A `Matrix4f` object containing 16 double-precision floating-point values that represent a 4x4 matrix.

The function calls `GL20.glUniformMatrix4()` to set the uniform matrix with the specified name and data. The `getLocation()` method is used to retrieve the location of the uniform in the shader program, and the `Util.createFlippedBuffer()` method is used to create a flipped buffer of the matrix data for efficient storage in the GPU memory.

items.name: unifromMat4

items.description: retrieves the location of a uniform in a program using the `GL20` class.

params.name: name
params.description: name of a uniform in the OpenGL program, which is used to locate the corresponding uniform in the program's layout.

returns.description: an integer representing the location of a uniform in a graphics program.

items.name: getLoaction

items.description: loads a shader program from a file and validates its structure.

params.name: filename
params.description: name of the shader file to be loaded.

returns.description:
a new `Shader` object representing the combined vertex and fragment shaders from the given file names.

* The output is of type `Shader`, which represents a shader program created by combining a vertex shader and a fragment shader.
* The shader program is represented by an integer ID assigned by GL20, which is a part of the Android API for graphics rendering.
* The shader program can be validated using the `validateProgram` function, which checks for errors in the program's structure or syntax.

Overall, the `loadShader` function creates and returns a shader program by combining two shaders and then validating the resulting program.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: creates a shader program, compiles and attaches it to a program in OpenGL.

params.name: target
params.description: type of shader to be created, which determines the functionality and structure of the generated shader code.

params.name: src
params.description: source code of the shader to be compiled.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D graphics program to which the loaded shader will be attached.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: validates a GPU program by linking and validating it with the OpenGL API.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D graphics program to be validated and linked with the GPU.

items.name: validateProgram

items.description: reads the contents of a file and returns its text content as a string.

params.name: file
params.description: file to read and return its contents as a String.

returns.description: a string containing the contents of the specified file.

items.name: getText

structured.description: a class `Model` that represents a 3D model in an OpenGL context. It provides a constructor to set the vertex array object (VAO) and the number of vertices, and a method `draw()` to render the model. The `enableAttribs()` and `disableAttribs()` methods enable and disable specific vertex attributes, respectively. The `getVAO()` and `getSize()` methods provide accessors for the VAO and size, respectively. Additionally, the code provides a factory method `load()` to load a 3D model from an array of vertices and an array of indices.

items.description: is used to handle vertex and index buffer data for rendering 3D models in OpenGL. It provides methods for binding and unbinding Vertex Array Objects (VAOs) and Element Array Buffers (EABs), as well as storing vertex and index data and enabling/disabling vertex attributtes. The class also provides a way to load pre-defined model data from an array of vertices and indices.
items.name: Model

items.description: binds a vertex array object, enables vertex attributes for position and texture coord, draws triangles using `glDrawElements`, disables vertex attributes, and unbinds the vertex array object.

usage.code:
Model model = Model.load(vertices, indices);
model.draw();

items.name: draw

items.description: enables two vertex attributes in a GL context using `glEnableVertexAttribArray`.
items.name: enableAttribs

items.description: disables both vertex attribute arrays for a specific GL context.
items.name: disableAttribs

items.description: returns the value of the variable `vao`.

returns.description: an integer value representing the VAO.

usage.code:
Model model = Model.load(vertices, indices);
model.getVAO(); // returns the VAO value

items.name: getVAO

items.description: retrieves the value of a field named `size`.
items.name: getSize

items.description: loads a 3D model from an array of vertices and an array of indices. It creates a Vertex Array Object (VAO), stores the indices, and then binds and unbinds the VAO. Finally, it returns a `Model` object representing the loaded model.

params.name: vertices
params.description:
3D geometric data of the model to be loaded, which is stored in an array of floating-point values.

* `vertices`: An array of floating-point values representing the 3D vertices of a model.
* `indices`: An array of integers representing the indices of the vertices in the model.

params.name: indices
params.description:
3D model's vertex indices, which are used to bind the vertices of the model to its corresponding geometry in the graphics pipeline.

* `indices`: An `int[]` array containing the indices of the vertices in the model.
* `v_count`: The number of vertices in the model, which can be calculated by summing up the lengths of all the `indices` arrays.

returns.description:
a `Model` object representing the loaded 3D model.

* `Model`: This is the type of the output, which is an instance of the `Model` class.
* `vao`: This is an integer variable representing the VAO (Vertex Array Object) handle created by the function.
* `v_count`: This is an integer variable representing the number of vertices in the model.

items.name: load

items.description: generates a new vertex array object (Vao) and binds it to the current context, allowing for its use in rendering graphics.

returns.description: an integer value representing the unique vertex array object (VAO) created.

items.name: createVAO

items.description: stores floating-point array data into a buffer object and sets vertex attributes for rendering.

params.name: attrib
params.description: 3D vertex attribute index that stores the data for the current buffer in the GPU's memory.

params.name: data
params.description:
2D array of floating-point values that will be stored in the vertex buffer object (VBO).

* `data`: An array of 4 float values representing vertex data.
* `attrib`: An integer representing the attribute index to which the vertex data will be mapped. There are two attribute indices in total, assigned using the `GL20.glVertexAttribPointer()` method.
* `vbo`: A buffer object handle generated by `GL15.glGenBuffers()`. The buffer is bound using `GL15.glBindBuffer(GL15.GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, vbo)` and data is transferred to it using `GL15.glBufferData(GL15.GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, Util.createFlippedBuffer(data), GL15.GL_STATIC_DRAW)`.

items.name: storeData

items.description: genrates a new buffer object and binds it to an element array buffer, then stores the provided integer array data in the buffer using the `glBufferData` method.

params.name: indices
params.description:
3D indices of the vertices of a geometric shape, which are stored in a buffer for later use in rendering.

* `indices` is an integer array representing a buffer containing indices for an element array buffer.
* The length of the array is specified by the size of the `indices` array, which is a variable passed as a parameter to the function.
* Each element in the `indices` array represents an index into a buffer for an element array, with each index being an integer value between 0 and the total number of elements in the buffer - 1, inclusive.

items.name: storeIndices

items.description: binds a vertex array object (VAO) using the `GL30.glBindVertexArray()` method, setting it to null.
items.name: unbindVAO

structured.description: A `Transform` class that handles transformations for objects in 3D space. It has various methods for rotating, looking at a point, and scaling an object, as well as getters and setters for its properties such as position, rotation, and scale. Additionally, it provides a way to update the transformation matrix based on changes to the object's position, rotation, or scale. The code also includes a `getParentMatrix()` method that returns the transformation matrix of the object's parent, if applicable.

items.description: has several fields and methods for manipulating transforms in a 3D space. The class has a `pos` field for storing the position of the transform, a `rot` field for storing the rotation of the transform, and a `scale` field for storing the scale of the transform. The class also has methods for updating the transform, rotating it, looking at a point in space, and getting the transformation matrix. Additionally, the class has a `parent` field for storing the transform's parent and a `getParentMatrix()` method for getting the parent transform's transformation matrix.
items.name: Transform

items.description: updates an object's position, rotation, and scale based on a provided input. If the input is not equal to the previous value, it sets the previous value as the new current value.

usage.description: In this example, a new instance of the Transform class is created, and its position, rotation, and scale are set to their default values using the setPos(), setRot(), and setScale() methods respectively. The update method is then called on the transformation object.

items.name: update

items.description: multiplies a quaternion representing a rotation axis and an angle, normalizes the result, and returns the new quaternion representation of the rotated orientation.

params.name: axis
params.description:
3D axis of rotation for the object being rotated.

* `axis`: A `Vector3f` object that represents the rotation axis in 3D space.
* `angle`: An `float` value representing the angle of rotation around the `axis`.

params.name: angle
params.description: 3D rotation angle about the specified `axis` of the object being rotated.

usage.code:
Transform myTransform = new Transform();
Vector3f axis = new Vector3f(1, 0, 0); // Rotate around the x-axis
float angle = (float) Math.PI;         // Rotate by pi radians
myTransform.rotate(axis, angle);       // Call the rotate method
usage.description:
The `axis` parameter is a Vector3f that indicates the axis of rotation. In this case, it is the x-axis. The `angle` parameter is a float that indicates the amount of rotation in radians. In this case, it is pi radians (180 degrees).

When called with these parameters, the rotate method will update the rotation Quaternion `rot` in myTransform to reflect the rotation around the x-axis by pi radians.

items.name: rotate

items.description: computes the rotation required to face a provided point while maintaining a specified direction as the "up" vector.

params.name: point
params.description:
3D position that the camera should look at.

* `point`: A `Vector3f` representing the point to look at in 3D space.
* `up`: A `Vector3f` representing the upward direction in 3D space, used for computing the rotation towards the point.

params.name: up
params.description:
3D direction opposite to which the camera's orientation should be rotated to look at the specified `point`.

* The `Vector3f` object `up` represents a 3D vector with x, y, and z components, which contain information about the direction of rotation.

items.name: lookAt

items.description: computes the rotation quaternion that looks at a given point from a specified up direction. The rotation is computed using a rotation matrix, which is created by multiplying a translation matrix and an orthonormal matrix.

params.name: point
params.description:
3D position that the look-at rotation is based on.

* `point`: A `Vector3f` object representing a 3D point in space. It has three attributes: `x`, `y`, and `z`, which represent the coordinates of the point along the x, y, and z axes, respectively.

params.name: up
params.description:
3D direction of the "up" vector in the local coordinate system, which is used to compute the rotation matrix for the `getLookAtRotation()` function.

* `up` is a `Vector3f` object that represents a vector in 3D space.
* `up` has three components: `x`, `y`, and `z`, which represent the coordinates of the vector in the x, y, and z axes, respectively.
* The magnitude of `up` is non-zero, indicating that it is not the zero vector.

The function returns a `Quaternion` object that represents the rotation necessary to look at the point `point` from the perspective of the `up` direction. This rotation is calculated using the `initRotation` method of the `Matrix4f` class, which takes the normalized difference between the point and the position (`pos`) as input and returns a rotation matrix.

returns.description:
a quaternion representing the rotation necessary to look at a point from a specific position and direction.

* The output is a Quaternion object that represents the rotation necessary to look at a point in 3D space from a specific position and up direction.
* The Quaternion is created by multiplying a rotation matrix (generated using the `Matrix4f` class) with the difference vector between the point and the position, normalized, and then adding the up vector.
* This resultant quaternion represents the rotation needed to face the lookAt direction from the current position.

usage.description:
In this example, the method getLookAtRotation takes two arguments: point and up, both of which are Vector3f types. The point argument represents a position in three-dimensional space that the object is looking at, and the up argument represents the direction that is considered "up" for the object's coordinate system.

The method returns a Quaternion type that represents the rotation needed to orient the object to look at the specified point from the current position of the object. The Quaternion type is a mathematical representation of a three-dimensional rotation, and it is used in computer graphics and other applications where 3D rotations need to be performed efficiently.

In this example, we create two new Vector3f objects: one for the point that the object is looking at (0, 0, -1), and another for the up direction of the object's coordinate system (0, 1, 0). Then, we pass these two arguments to the getLookAtRotation method. The method returns a Quaternion type that represents the rotation needed to orient the object to look at the specified point from the current position of the object. We then assign this returned Quaternion type to the variable lookAtRot.

We can then use the lookAtRot variable to perform rotations on the object using the quaternion multiplication operator, or by passing it to methods that accept a Quaternion parameter.

items.name: getLookAtRotation

items.description: evaluates if any of its arguments have changed, returning `true` if they have and `false` otherwise. It compares the values of `parent`, `pos`, `rot`, and `scale` to their previous values stored in `old` variables.

returns.description: a boolean value indicating whether the object has changed in any of its properties.

items.name: hasChanged

items.description: combines a translation, rotation, and scaling matrix to create a single transformation matrix. It takes the position, rotation, and scale of an object as inputs and returns the transformed matrix.

returns.description:
a matrix representing a transformation from the parent matrix, consisting of translation, rotation, and scaling components.

The returned matrix is a 4x4 homogeneous transformation matrix, which represents a 3D transformation consisting of translation, rotation, and scaling. The translation component represents the movement of the object in the world space, while the rotation component describes the orientation of the object around its center. The scaling component determines the size of the object in each dimension.

The `getParentMatrix()` method is called first to obtain the transformation matrix of the parent node, which is then multiplied with the translation, rotation, and scaling matrices to produce the final transformation matrix. This process reflects the hierarchical structure of the scene graph, where the transformation of a node is dependent on its parent node's transformation.

usage.description: In this example, a new Transform instance is created and its position, rotation, and scale are set using the respective set methods. Then, the getTransformation method is called to obtain the transformation matrix for the transform object. The resulting matrix can then be used to transform objects or perform other operations on the transform's position, rotation, and scale.

items.name: getTransformation

items.description: retrieves the transformation matrix of the parent node in a hierarchical graph and returns it.

returns.description:
a Matrix4f representation of the parent transformation matrix.

* `parentMatrix`: This is a `Matrix4f` object representing the parent transformation matrix of the current transform.
* `hasChanged()`: A boolean method indicating whether the parent transformation matrix has changed since the last call to `getParentMatrix`. If the matrix has changed, the function will return the updated matrix.

items.name: getParentMatrix

items.description: sets the `parent` field to a given `Transform` object, providing a new parent node for the current node in a hierarchical structure.

params.name: parent
params.description:
Transform to which the current instance will be added as a child transform.

* Type: `Transform` - This class represents a transform in a graph.
* Fields: `transformId`, `parent`, `child` - These fields contain information about the transform's identity and relationships with other transforms in the graph.

usage.code:
Transform parent = new Transform(); //create a parent transform
Transform child = new Transform(); //create a child transform
child.setParent(parent); //set the child's parent to the parent transform

items.name: setParent

items.description: takes a `Vector3f` object `pos` and applies a transformation to it using the parent matrix, returning the transformed position as another `Vector3f` object.

returns.description:
a transformed position vector based on the matrix provided by its parent matrix.

* The `Vector3f` object represents the transformed position of an entity after applying a matrix transformation.
* The transformation is performed by calling the `transform` method on the parent matrix, passing in the original position vector as its argument.
* The resulting transformed position vector contains the position of the entity after applying the specified transformation.

usage.code:
Transform myObject = new Transform();
myObject.setParent(parent); // set the parent transform
myObject.setPos(new Vector3f(1, 2, 3)); // set position of object
Vector3f transformedPosition = myObject.getTransformedPos(); // get the transformed position

items.name: getTransformedPos

items.description: transforms a given rotation quaternion `rot` by multiplying it with the transformation quaternion of its parent object, if available, and returns the result.

returns.description:
a transformed Quaternion representing the combination of the parent's rotation and the provided rotation.

The `Quaternion` object returned by the function represents the transformed rotation of the parent node relative to the original rotation. The quaternion is a mathematical representation of a 3D rotation that can be used to rotate objects in 3D space.

The first component of the quaternion (1, 0, 0, 0) represents the scale factor of the transformation. The second component (0) indicates that no translation was applied. The third component (0, 0, 0) represents the rotation axis, which is the direction of the rotation. The fourth component (0) indicates that the rotation angle is zero, meaning that no rotation was applied.

Therefore, the returned quaternion represents the identity transformation, which means that no rotation or translation has been applied to the parent node relative to its original position and orientation.

usage.description: In this example, we create a new Transform instance and set its position and rotation to (0, 1, 0) and (0, 1, 0, 0) respectively. We then call the getTransformedRot method on the transform instance and store the result in a Quaternion variable. Finally, we print out the transformed rotation using the println statement.

items.name: getTransformedRot

items.description: returns the position of an object in a 3D space as a `Vector3f` object.

returns.description:
a reference to a `Vector3f` object representing the position of the entity.

The `Vector3f` object returned by the function represents the position of an entity in 3D space, consisting of x, y, and z components. The x component represents the horizontal position, y component represents the vertical position, and the z component represents the depth or height position. Each component has a floating-point value between -1 and 1, which can be used to represent a wide range of positions within the 3D space.

items.name: getPos

items.description: sets the position of an object to a provided Vector3f value.

params.name: pos
params.description:
3D position of an object and is assigned to the `pos` field of the class instance, updating its internal state.

* The `Vector3f` class has three properties: `x`, `y`, and `z`, which represent the coordinates of the position in 3D space.
* Each property is a `float` value representing the coordinate value.
* The `Vector3f` class provides methods for adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing its properties, as well as for calculating the magnitude (length) of the vector.

items.name: setPos

items.description: adds a `Vector3f` object to the current position of an entity, by setting the position to the sum of the current position and the input vector.

params.name: addVec
params.description:
3D vector to be added to the current position of the object, which is then updated by calling the `setPos()` method with the resulting new position.

* `Vector3f`: This is the class that represents a 3D vector in Java, with three attributes: `x`, `y`, and `z`.
* `add Vec`: This method takes another `Vector3f` object as input and adds its values to the current position of the object.

items.name: addToPos

items.description: returns a `Quaternion` object representing the rotation of an entity.

returns.description:
a Quaternion object representing the current rotation of the entity.

The return value is of type Quaternion, which represents an object that encodes rotation in 3D space as a combination of real and imaginary parts. The Quaternion class is part of the Java Math library.

usage.code:
Transform transform = new Transform();
Quaternion rot = transform.getRot();

items.name: getRot

items.description: sets the rotational orientation of an object by assigning a Quaternion value to the `rot` field of the class.

params.name: rotation
params.description:
4D quaternion value that specifies the rotation of the object to which the method is called, and it is assigned to the `rot` field of the class.

* `Quaternion rotation`: This class represents a quaternion-based rotation in 3D space. It contains various attributes such as `x`, `y`, `z`, and `w`, which represent the real and imaginary parts of the quaternion, respectively. These attributes can be used to manipulate the rotation matrix.

items.name: setRot

items.description: returns a `Vector3f` object representing the scale of the game object.

returns.description:
a `Vector3f` object representing the current scale of the game object.

* The output is a `Vector3f` object that represents the scale of the game object.
* The vector consists of three elements: x, y, and z, which represent the scaling factors in the respective dimensions.
* Each element of the vector has a value between 0 and 1, indicating the amount of scaling applied to the object in each dimension.

usage.code:
Transform t = new Transform();
Vector3f scale = t.getScale();

items.name: getScale

items.description: sets the scale of an object, updating its `scale` field with the provided value.

params.name: scale
params.description:
3D vector that determines the scaling of the object.

The `Vector3f` class in Java represents a 3D vector with three elements, representing x, y, and z components, respectively. Therefore, `scale` is a 3D vector that contains the scalar values for each component.

By assigning the input `scale` to the field `this.scale`, the function effectively modifies the instance variable `scale` of the class `this`.

items.name: setScale

items.description: returns an empty string.

returns.description:
an empty string.

The function returns an empty string ("") as its output. This indicates that the object does not have any additional information to display when it is converted into a string.

The absence of any content in the returned string suggests that the object has no attributes or properties to display. As a result, the `toString` function provides minimal information about the object.

items.name: toString
